transcript.clean_text: what would be the abiding characteristic of day two on the uae tour wind and plenty of it an effective sandstorm was blowing out there as they went out into the desert and indeed it made life difficult for a breakaway who were brave enough to take this on two intermediate sprints but inevitably a dash for the line at the abu dhabi breakwater after a distance of 176 kilometers plenty of loose sand upon the road to welcome gasprom rus vello three of their number took on the break kachekov who was in the break on the opening day along with strzok who'd taken the maximum points in the intermediate sprints and he was in the mood to enhance his lead in that black jersey on this day kirkland the third of the gasprom men out front the czech champion but the black jersey it was that just continued to assemble points and looks unbeatable in that competition well as for the chase in a block headwind they weren't much minded to actually put in too much stress to the day although some was generated with the usual brain fade when the bunch is riding slowly together some bumps and bruises then for francis de geas clem on devi a burger on his backside as he got himself back in and everyone else i was kind of wondering when this headwind would end the answer was with around 25 kilometers to go but until that point it almost looked like a sunday afternoon so relaxed was it we were wondering about the pace and the answer came from our leader jasper phillips said about running pace well it would get an awful lot quicker after the turn a block head wind was suddenly going to change to a crosswind and with the high buildings offering up some protection on the run for home really it was all about the final corner there would be a race to it twelve hundred meters from home a significant right-hander and if you're in the right place up towards the front you had a chance everyone knew it and they were investing some a little bit too early group armor francis de gea were there likewise team bike exchange was starting to mob the road with 10 to go india's grenadiers getting themselves into position no sign really of alps in phoenix and none either of quickstep alpha they were keeping their light under a bushel until the very end well with a big highway about to compress into a very distinct right-hander israel premier tech bossed it into the corner as we emerged all of a sudden frances de joe was still intact mark cavenish was also in the frame jesper phillips in his red jersey screened at this point and looking very good indeed sam bennett well bennett was boxed in cav took to the front phillipson and his red jersey suddenly emerged as well and in the end it was a two-up sprint for the line and mark cavenish was to take it fantastic form by the manxman absolute delight for him and upset for a few others demar not with the happiest of endings to a busy day third home pascal ackermann for uae team emirates while wondering what he might have done not much against mark cavendish in this kind of mood he taking it ahead of phillips and ackermann corey up there demar rounding out the top five kav as usual offering up his thanks immediately to those who'd helped none more effectively than michael murku phillipson still leads ahead of struck off with his bonus seconds assembled yet again out in the break tomorrow it's time trial time however phillipson well it may well be he hands over the jersey we shall see in the nine kilometer run around ajiman you
